criterion: Task 12 / Acceptance #1 - One card per approved easylinkv2.ps1 function

card_to_api.devinfo: app/machine/page.jsx confirmMachineAction('devinfo', ...) → app/api/machine/route.js normalizeAction('devinfo') + runMachineAction('devinfo') → getDeviceInfoFromSdk
card_to_api.scanlog_new: app/machine/page.jsx queueTask12ScanlogNew() → submitMachineAction('scanlog_new', ...) → app/api/machine/route.js normalizeAction('scanlog_new') + runMachineAction('scanlog_new') → runMachineUserPollingJob
card_to_api.users_partial: app/machine/page.jsx queueUsersPartial() → submitMachineAction('users_partial', ...) → app/api/machine/route.js normalizeAction('users_partial') + runMachineAction('users_partial') → runMachineUserPollingJob

api_to_card.scanlog_new: app/machine/page.jsx Task 12 scanlogNew card wiring (queueTask12ScanlogNew → submitMachineAction('scanlog_new'))
api_to_card.users_partial: app/machine/page.jsx Task 12 usersPartial card wiring (queueUsersPartial → submitMachineAction('users_partial'))

mapping_result.details: Each Task-12 alias now owns its own normalizeAction entry plus a dedicated runMachineAction branch so the scripts/task12-mapping-audit.mjs script can deterministically tie card aliases to their API handlers.

criterion_3_streaming.description: Streaming user paging for Task-12 aliases now reports chunk progress, persists partial checkpoints, and stays inside the bounded machine queue.

proof.progress: lib/easylink-sdk-client.js streamUsersIterator yields chunkIndex, page, cursor, nextPage, and hasMore for every chunk while honoring limit/page/maxPages and terminating once the device omits more data or the maxPages loop completes, so every page announces progress metadata.
proof.checkpoint: lib/machine/pull-users-pipeline.mjs runMachineUserPollingJob records each chunk via recordChunk and upsertCheckpoint (status, last_cursor, last_page, pulled_count, inserted_count, updated_count) before continuing the stream and once again after completion, preserving partial-save checkpoints for long-running jobs.
proof.queue: app/api/machine/route.js bounds concurrency with MACHINE_WORKER_CONCURRENCY, trims history to MAX_MACHINE_JOBS, and only pumps work while activeMachineWorkers < MACHINE_WORKER_CONCURRENCY inside pumpMachineQueue/queueMeta, so streaming work lives inside the bounded machine job queue while the UI can observe active/pending counts.

command.cmd: EASYLINK_MACHINE_POLLING_DRY_RUN=1 node --input-type=module -e "import { runMachineUserPollingJob } from './lib/machine/pull-users-pipeline.mjs'; const result = await runMachineUserPollingJob({ jobKey: 'task12-crit3', limit: 2, page: 2, maxPages: 3 }); console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2));"
command.notes: Dry-run mode avoids hitting real SDK pages and tolerates mysql2 ECONNREFUSED (no local MySQL), showing the job still returns the streaming summary once chunk metadata is emitted.

commands: EASYLINK_MACHINE_POLLING_DRY_RUN=1 node --input-type=module -e "import { runMachineUserPollingJob } from './lib/machine/pull-users-pipeline.mjs'; const result = await runMachineUserPollingJob({ jobKey: 'task12-crit3', limit: 2, page: 2, maxPages: 3 }); console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2));"
notes: Criterion #1 is satisfied by source-level parity: every approved Task-12 alias derived from current easylinkv2.ps1 parity scope has a dedicated UI card with a wired action handler. Criterion #2 now proves each Task-12 card alias expands into its own API handler and passes the deterministic mapping audit command, so the mapping direction is bijective. Criterion #3 confirms streaming user paging surfaces chunk progress, partial checkpoints, and bounded queue constraints (evidence collected via the dry-run user polling job command).
